definition: someone who is attached to an organisation or group of people is working with them
example: Emma is a researcher attached to the University of Manchester

definition: if you attend an event, you go to it and are present there
example: all students are welcome to attend
school attendance is above the national average

definition: if you are based in a particular place, you work and/or live there
example: I’ll be based in Newcastle until March

definition: if you are into something, you are interested in it and enjoy it very much
example: what kind of music are you into?

definition: a civil servant is someone who works for a government department. All the people and departments together are called the civil service
example: she’s a senior civil servant in London
I’ve applied for a job in the civil service

definition: a current situation, job, home, etc is the situation, job, home, etc that is happening, being done, or being used now
example: when did you move to your current home?
the road is currently being repaired

definition: if a situation or activity involves something, it includes it as a necessary part
example: the job may involve working at weekends

definition: something that is official is approved by the government or someone in authority
example: French is an official language in Cameroon
the club is now officially open

definition: an only child is someone who doesn’t have any brothers or sisters
example: he was an only child of wealthy parents

definition: someone who is patient is able to do something that takes a long time without losing interest or becoming angry or upset
example: thanks for waiting, you’ve been very patient
collocations: be patient with someone or something

example: sorry, but I’m beginning to lose my patience
they waited patiently until the judge arrived
as time passed, he became more and more impatient
the captain shook his head impatiently

definition: if you present information, you describe it to other people in a formal way. If you are presenting at a conference, workshop, etc, you are giving a talk there
example: which conferences are you presenting at?
he gave a really interesting presentation on new words
the first presenter spoke so quietly we could hardly hear him

definition: if two people are separated, they are married but no longer live with each other
example: my parents are separated
they decided to separate, and Alan moved out last weekend
within two years he’d been though marriage, separation and divorce

definition: a session is a period of time during which a class or meeting takes place
example: each session lasts about 45 minutes

definition: software is programs used by computers. Computer equipment is called hardware computer software
example: it takes a few hours to download the software

definition: someone’s strengths are the qualities and abilities that they have which make them successful
example: her strengths include good IT and communication skills
she’s always been strong at music and drama

definition: a translator is a person who changes spoken or written words into another language, especially as their job
example: the only way we could communicate was through a translator
his book has been translated into 50 different languages
the original translation was completed in 1609

definition: a warehouse is a large building where goods are stored before they are sold or moved to the shops
example: we’ll need to pick it up from the warehouse

definition: someone’s weaknesses are the things that they are not very good at
example: her lack of patience is a major weakness
I hated science and was particularly weak at chemistry

definition: an accent is the way someone says words and shows what country or region they come from
example: she speaks with an American accent

definition: something that is accurate is correct or true in every detail
example: this is a more accurate translation

definition: adjective:  inaccurate
example: he accurately calculated the distance from the Earth to the Sun

definition: adjective:  inaccurate
example: the teacher monitors both fluency and accuracy

definition: an achievement is something good that you have done, usually after a lot of time and effort
example: congratulations, it’s a remarkable achievement
it’s remarkable what we’ve managed to achieve

definition: your ambition is your strong wish to become successful at what you do, or to achieve a particular thing
example: her ambition was to become a doctor
a highly ambitious junior doctor
he ambitiously attempted to walk across the continent
he led a quiet and unambitious life

definition: if you appreciate something, you understand why it is good or special
example: students should be taught to appreciate the benefits of learning a language

definition: an appreciation for/of something
example: Felix had a deep appreciation for scientific research

definition: the basics are the most important parts of something that you should learn
example: an introduction to the basics of computer programming
you can pick up the basic skills from watching online videos

definition: a central question, idea, aspect, etc is one of the most important questions, ideas, aspects, etc that is used or needed
example: a central question of the book is whether these skills can be acquired over time

definition: someone or something that is efficient produces good results without wasting time or effort
example: the new system is far more efficient than the old one
the whole system is now operating more efficiently
they adopted new measures to improve efficiency
the old farming methods were increasingly inefficient
we can’t afford to run the business inefficiently
the government was accused of inefficiency and corruption

definition: someone who is embarrassed is ashamed of something and worried about what other people will think of them
example: we were embarrassed about the lack of food
they were embarrassed by the entire incident
can you stop asking embarrassing questions?
she is such an embarrassment to her parents

definition: evidence is facts that show if something is true
example: no evidence of criminal activity has ever been found

definition: if you exaggerate, you say that something is bigger, better or more extreme than it really is
example: these newspaper reports are always grossly exaggerated
OK, I admit, that was a slight exaggeration

definition: if you can speak a language fluently, you can speak it very well
example: the owners are German and speak English fluently
I am fluent in spoken and written English
the teacher monitors both fluency and accuracy

definition: someone who is frustrated feels angry or upset because they are being prevented from achieving something
example: she gets frustrated when she can’t express herself
his constant questions frustrated me
having to wait so long is incredibly frustrating
I understand your frustration at this situation

definition: genes are the parts of a cell that control physical growth, development, etc
example: no single gene is responsible for the cancer

definition: genes are the parts of a cell that control physical growth, development, etc. A disease or medical condition that is genetic is connected to someone’s genes
example: the illness is caused by a genetic defect
they don’t grow genetically modified crops
he studied genetics in his first year of college

definition: if you can get by, you have just enough of something, for example knowledge or money, so that you can do what you need to do
example: I’m not fluent in Spanish, but I can get by

definition: if you give up, you stop trying to do something because you think it is too difficult
example: there’s only another week to go, you can’t give up now

definition: global means including or affecting the whole world. Globalisation is the idea that improved technology and communications are causing the world to develop a single culture and economy
example: globalisation has transformed the way we do business
the global economic crisis

definition: to grow means to get larger. A growing amount is an amount that is increasing. A growing situation, feeling, etc is a situation, feeling, etc that is starting to exist more often, and is becoming more important or serious
example: a growing number of pupils are choosing not to learn a foreign language

definition: if you lack something, you do not have it, or do not have enough of it, although it would be useful if you did
example: she lacked the necessary skills
there was a lack of evidence
his manager was lacking in any personal skills

definition: a legend is a famous person who many people admire
example: the Italian priest, Cardinal Mezzofanti, is a legend among linguists
the legendary rock band Queen

definition: if something is limited, there is not very much of it, or it is not as good as it might be
example: they have very limited ambitions
the report has strengths as well as limitations (parts which are not clear or correct)

definition: if you master something, you learn it thoroughly so that you can do it very well
example: when you’ve mastered this you can try something more difficult

definition: if someone does their job well or badly, you can say they are a good or bad performer
example: our top performers can expect a big pay rise this year
she was able to perform well under pressure
poor job performance is a typical result of stress

definition: if you pick up something or pick something up, you learn a new skill over a period of time and without a lot of effort
example: she picked up some Spanish when she was living in Madrid

definition: if you possess a quality or ability, you have it
example: an effective leader can possess many different character traits

definition: when a computer processes information, it uses the information in order to do a particular task. When your brain processes information, it takes in sound, speech or other information and organises it so that you understand it
example: humans have a limited ability to process information
my thought processing was much slower when I was hungry

definition: if you struggle, you try hard to do something you find difficult
example: Anna has always struggled with maths
it was a struggle to understand her when she spoke so fast

definition: something that is sufficient is as much as is needed for a particular purpose
example: there is sufficient evidence to believe he could speak many languages
she had sufficiently recovered to make the trip to Australia
the project was cancelled due to insufficient funds

definition: the top person or thing is the most important or best one of their kind
example: she was a top performer

definition: if someone acts as a particular sort of person, they do what that sort of person is expected to do for a while
example: we often need local people to act as guides
he is the acting governor (not the real one, but doing the job until the real one returns)

definition: someone’s behaviour is the way they normally do and say things
example: they changed their behaviour as a result of the experience
he’s been behaving very strangely this week

definition: on a ship, the cabins are the rooms where the passengers and the members of the crew live and sleep during a journey
example: she worked as a cabin cleaner

definition: if you catch what someone is saying, you hear it and understand it. If you don’t catch what they are saying, you don’t hear it properly and do not understand what they mean
example: they spoke so fast I didn’t catch anything at all

definition: to come up to someone means to come towards them
example: two Chinese guys came up to me
collocations: come up to someone or something

definition: a crew is a group of people with special skills who all work together doing the same job
example: a member of the film crew

definition: the engine of a vehicle is the part that produces the power and that makes the vehicle move
example: he worked in the engine room of a cruise ship

definition: when people are filming, they are using cameras and recording equipment to make a film for television or the cinema
example: we were filming in Sumatra

definition: a gesture is a movement of your hand or arm which you do in order to help make yourself understood, often at the same time as you are speaking
example: using mostly gesture, I explained that they were in the wrong street
she gestured to me to come in

definition: a misunderstanding happens when someone doesn’t understand something properly, often causing some confusion
example: it was all a complete misunderstanding
don’t misunderstand me – I didn’t mean it was wrong

definition: a mixture is a number of different things all together in the same place
example: I communicated with a mixture of Chinese and gesture
if you mix yellow and blue you get green
if you mix yellow and blue you get green

definition: if something motivates you, it gives you the enthusiasm and determination you need to do something
example: their teacher really knows how to motivate them
children are highly motivated learners of language
greed was a motivating factor in his decision
he has no motivation to study
the motive behind the attack is unclear

definition: an overnight activity is one that starts in the evening and continues through the night until the next morning
example: my dad took an overnight train to Prague
we flew overnight to New York

definition: someone’s papers are official documents such as a passport or identity card that prove who they are
example: a policeman asked him for his papers

definition: if you rush, you do something or go somewhere quickly because you are in a hurry
example: we had to rush her to the doctor (take her to the doctor very quickly)
I’m in a rush (I don’t have much time)

definition: a search is an attempt to find something. If you go in search of something, you look for it and try to find it search
example: we go all over the place in search of unusual wild animals
collocations: go in search of someone or something
a search for someone or something

example: they searched my bag at the airport

definition: if you settle somewhere, you start to live there permanently
example: they chose to settle in Rio
European settlers started to arrive in 1793
